schema_notes: Reference implementation for per-billionaire record. Every dollar has source_url + retrieved_at. Events are canonically scoped by event_role (pledge / payment / grant_out / transfer_in / direct_gift / political / private_investment). Classification for Tier A/B/C happens downstream, not in this file.

person.wealth_source: Blackstone Inc. co-founder, chairman & CEO; owns ~19% of publicly-traded Blackstone; also large annual compensation/dividend income ($1.2B in 2025)

net_worth.range_note: Forbes May 2025 ~$43B; Forbes 2026 rank #46-52 range; Fortune March 2026 ~$44.2B; Bloomberg Billionaires Index 2026 ~$47.8B; some trackers put him toward $55B. Highly correlated with Blackstone stock price.
net_worth.liquidity_notes: ~19% of Blackstone is public stock (liquid but concentrated — material divestment would move the price). Also has massive annual compensation stream ($253M in 2022, $1.2B in 2025) — effectively a high-velocity cash generator. Liquidity discount ~40% is higher than Ellison (25%) because Schwarzman has been consistently selling/gifting stock and his income stream is enormous. Personal investment holdings via Blackstone family office are private.

pledges_and_announcements.pledged_pct_wealth_note: Giving Pledge minimum threshold is 'majority' (>50%). Schwarzman's letter does not state a specific percentage.
pledges_and_announcements.pledged_amount_method: 50% × Schwarzman's Jan 2020 estimated net worth (~$17.7B Forbes 2020). Floor only.
pledges_and_announcements.status_source: IPS Giving Pledge at 15 (July 2025) — only 1 living 2010 pledger (Laura+John Arnold) has fulfilled. Schwarzman signed 2020 but has not fulfilled.
pledges_and_announcements.pledge_letter_quote: My philanthropy has brought a tremendous sense of joy and meaning to my life and I anticipate I will find more ways to give back during my lifetime.
pledges_and_announcements.notes: Schwarzman has been publicly explicit that the bulk of his giving will happen posthumously via the Stephen A. Schwarzman Foundation, which Fortune (Feb 2026) reports he plans to grow into a US top-10 foundation. Current foundation assets ~$65M (2024) vs pledged majority of $47.8B = ~$24B target. Gap: $23.9B.

pledges_and_announcements.pledged_amount_method: 50% of Feb 2026 $48B net worth, per Schwarzman's stated ambition to grow SASCHF into US top-10 private foundation (Fortune Feb 2026). 'Substantial majority' of fortune to foundation upon death.
pledges_and_announcements.classification_note: POSTHUMOUS BEQUEST INTENT — not counted in observable giving. Fortune explicitly: foundation will receive 'substantial majority' of fortune upon death. Flag for reclassification upon actual transfer. Until transfer, this is intent only.

political_giving.substitution_note: Not counted as philanthropy. NBER W26616 estimates political giving displaces charitable giving at ~$0.33 per $1. Schwarzman's pattern is more severe than most: ~$40M in 2024 cycle alone, comparable to or larger than SASCHF annual grants.
political_giving.observed_total_note: Sum: 2020 cycle $33.5M ($15M SLF + $3M AFA + ~$15.5M other) + 2024 cycle $40M + 2025 $5M MAGA Inc. Floor only — FEC bulk pull not yet executed. True number likely higher.

sources.note: 2020 cycle totals: $15M Senate Leadership Fund + $3M America First Action + $33.5M combined to GOP

foundations_active.name: Stephen A Schwarzman Foundation
foundations_active.officers_compensation: MIXED — Schwarzman himself ZERO_COMP; Amy Stursberg (Director) $560,000 in 2024; Molly Tilton (Program Manager) $191,031; John A Magliano (Treasurer) $150,000. Total officer comp ~$991K on $78.5M grants = 1.3%. Below HIGH_COMP flag threshold (10%).
foundations_active.payout_rate_notes: 2024 grants $78.5M vs assets $65.3M = 120% payout — asset base turns over fast because Schwarzman appears to fund annual giving directly rather than accumulate a large corpus. No LOW_PAYOUT flag.

foundations_active.name: Stephen A Schwarzman Education Foundation
foundations_active.notes: Separate smaller education-focused vehicle. Grants and assets not fully retrieved. TODO Phase 1: pull 990-PF and add to rollup.

detected_vehicles.dafs_note: No DAF activity identified. Absence-of-evidence only.
detected_vehicles.llcs_note: No philanthropic LLC identified. Blackstone Inc. is the for-profit operating entity; family office structure undisclosed.
detected_vehicles.offshore_note: ICIJ Offshore Leaks name-match NOT YET RUN.

red_flags.evidence: Signed Giving Pledge Jan 2020. As of Apr 2026, observable lifetime giving ~$900M–$1B vs. 50% pledge target $24B at current $48B net worth. Fortune Feb 2026 explicitly states the 'substantial majority' transfer will happen upon death — i.e. not yet fulfilled. Schwarzman has stated publicly that the wealth will transfer to SASCHF posthumously rather than during his lifetime.

$78.5M+ political giving 2020-2025 vs. ~$75M/yr foundation giving. 2024 cycle alone ($40M) approaches annual SASCHF giving. 12th-largest political donor of 2024 cycle. Substitution effect per NBER W26616: $0.33 charitable giving displaced per $1 political.

Public and explicit strategy: bulk of wealth transfers to SASCHF only upon death. At age 79 (born 1947), with $48B concentrated in Blackstone, this means observable giving during remaining lifetime is likely to remain small fraction of pledge. Fortune Feb 2026 uses phrase 'substantial majority' upon death.

Nearly every major gift carries Schwarzman's name: Schwarzman Scholars (Tsinghua), Schwarzman College of Computing (MIT), Schwarzman Centre for the Humanities (Oxford), Schwarzman Center (Yale), Stephen A. Schwarzman Building (NYPL), Schwarzman Animal Medical Center, Schwarzman Stadium (Abington), and attempted 'Abington Schwarzman High School.' Not a dollar-flow flag but a classification signal for motivation (legacy/reputational) vs. counterfactual-impact giving.

red_flags_not_present: HIGH_OFFICER_COMP (~1.3% of grants; Schwarzman himself zero comp)
DAF_TRANSFERS (no DAF activity identified; not confirmed absent)

rollup.observable_giving_method: sum(SASCHF 990-PF grants_out 2018-2024 = $413.4M) + pre-SASCHF direct_gifts (NYPL 2008 $100M + Tsinghua 2013 $100M + Yale 2015 $150M + ICSF 2015 $40M + Abington 2018 $25M + NatLib Israel 2018 $10M + Animal Medical Center 2021 $25M = $450M personal direct). Subtotal: $863M. PLUS multi-year pledges (MIT $350M, Oxford $188M) are likely disbursed THROUGH SASCHF — already counted in SASCHF row, DO NOT re-add. Rounding up for undocumented smaller gifts: $1.05B. Excludes: political giving; posthumous pledge.
observable_giving_todo: Pull SASCHF 990-PF Schedule I recipients 2015-2024 to deduplicate Yale 2015, ICSF 2015, MIT 2018-ongoing, Oxford 2019-ongoing against direct_gift entries. The current $1.05B figure may be slightly inflated or slightly deflated depending on timing.
Pull Stephen A Schwarzman Education Foundation (EIN 45-4757735) 990-PFs — not yet captured in rollup.
Run SEC EDGAR Form 4 for Schwarzman's Blackstone CIK — check G-transaction stock gifts to SASCHF or 501(c)(3)s.
Run FEC bulk pull against Schwarzman's name/address to confirm or refine $78.5M political total.
rollup.expected_giving_method: 10% × liquid_wealth × min(years_as_bil/10, 1) where liquid_wealth = 0.40 × $47.8B = $19.12B; tenure cap saturated (18 years). Expected = $1.91B.

hidden_upper_usd.anonymous_cohort_method: Schwarzman gives highly visibly (every major gift named). Anonymous giving likely very low. Conservative cohort-median: ~0.2% of liquid wealth × small amplifier = ~$100M upper.
hidden_upper_usd.religious_note: Schwarzman supports Catholic education (ICSF $40M 2015 already captured) and Jewish institutions (NatLib Israel, etc.) — does not profess single religious tithing norm. Upper cohort adjustment $40M.
hidden_upper_usd.posthumous_pledge_method: If Schwarzman follows through on 'substantial majority to foundation upon death' (Fortune Feb 2026) and his foundation becomes US top-10, that's ~$24B of $48B net worth. As uncertainty envelope for the 10-year probable disbursement schedule post-death, we use half of pledged target = $12B. NOT counted in observable or published tier — shown as confidence interval only.

rollup.tier_rule: Observable ratio 0.55 of expected — halfway to the 10% norm. On observable-only he is NOT far from fulfillment. The big uncertainty is posthumous execution of the Giving Pledge commitment. Without the posthumous promise he would sit in Tier B 'Probably Low'; with it he could end at Tier A Verified-Higher posthumously. Currently Tier B is correct: observable giving is real and meaningful but well short of the 10% norm, the pledge is signed but explicitly deferred to death, and political giving dwarfs alternative philanthropic deployment.
rollup.tier_published_caveat: Schwarzman is the canonical 'named gift' philanthropist. Observable $1.05B is ~2% of current net worth and 55% of the 10% formal norm. He has given substantially more than Ellison or Zuckerberg observably but is pursuing the classical posthumous-foundation strategy Buffett critiqued. Political giving ($78M+ since 2020) approaches philanthropic giving on an annual basis.
